action.reasoning: Observation: Robot reaches human (dist 0.65-1.36m) but angle stays -1.7 to -2.6 rad (human behind/right). Robot spins at -10 ang_vel but angle doesn't decrease. Analysis: The human is moving and the robot can't track fast enough. Also, when dist < 2m and angle is large, I need to spin faster (up to 20) AND move forward slightly to circle around. The facing threshold in the config needs checking - let me use a more aggressive approach. Key fix: when close to human, use maximum angular velocity AND add forward motion to circle around. Also need to handle the case where human is behind by doing a tight circle. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with: 1) Max angular velocity 20 when close, 2) Circle maneuver (forward + turn) when close but not facing, 3) Submit eval immediately.
